title: xeraea eriophylla
intro: Xeraea eriophylla, also known as the Mormon tea or ceanothus, is a flowering shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Xeraea eriophylla include treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as digestive problems and fever. The herbal preparations of Xeraea eriophylla can be made into a tea by infusing the leaves in hot water, or by using the roots to make a decoction that can be used to treat various health conditions.
